pi_items.source: WebSearch confirmed Atenolol is an FDA-approved beta-blocker medication (MedlinePlus, Mayo Clinic, NCBI, Drugs.com sources); setup_task.sh line 44 inserts 'Atenolol 50mg' with verified standard dosage from FDA
setup_task.sh line 22 explicitly creates demographic record with first_name='Maria'
setup_task.sh line 22 explicitly creates demographic record with last_name='Santos'
verifier.py line 32 retrieves this metadata value; line 72 checks if 'recall' is present in message text
verifier.py line 33 retrieves this metadata value; line 72 checks if 'stop' is present in message text
setup_oscar.sh comment states 'Default login: oscardoc / oscar / PIN: 1117'; setup_task.sh line 41 references provider_no '999998' as oscardoc default
setup_oscar.sh comment confirms 'Default login: oscardoc / oscar / PIN: 1117'; docker commands use '-poscar' flag indicating this password for application database user
setup_oscar.sh comment explicitly states 'PIN: 1117' as the default login PIN for OSCAR EMR

privileged_info_summary: The task involves identifying and flagging a patient (Maria Santos, ID 501) currently prescribed Atenolol—a real, FDA-approved beta-blocker medication with standard dosing of 50mg. The setup script synthetically creates both the patient and prescription in a test OSCAR EMR database. The task requires creating a tickler (reminder) with message containing 'Recall' and 'Stop', and setting priority to High. OSCAR EMR login credentials are oscardoc/oscar/PIN:1117. The task verifier checks database records for tickler creation, message content accuracy, and priority level via three scoring criteria totaling 80 points for completion.
